findings.problem: The chapter's advertised five-way fracture partially collapses here. Lola's objection is delivered as a clean summary of her day/night split, and Earl's supposed verdict mostly paraphrases her "we knew who we were helping" logic. Two different characters end up doing the same rhetorical work in the center of the confrontation, which weakens both idiolect separation and the chapter's promised moral split.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Lola's objection at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:137 so it sounds like Lola under stress: faster, braided, self-correcting, and tied to Tammy/daytime-nighttime pressure without turning into a neat thesis statement. Rebuild Earl's intervention at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149 so it lands as his own terse collective-trust or solidarity verdict rather than a restatement of Lola's point. Use the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:139 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:151 to show the room registering two genuinely different objections.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:137 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149, a cold reader must be able to identify Lola and Earl by syntax and premise alone: Lola's speech should loop, self-correct, or braid pressure through story-adjacent language, while Earl's should be one hard, spare verdict grounded in collective self-protection. If Earl's line can still be swapped with Lola's objection without changing the scene's logic, fail.

findings.problem: Harry's closing admonition, "If you report, make sure it's because you need to. Not because you're angry," reads like polished motive-summary rather than Harry's established ledger-like register. It turns him into a tidy moral diagnostician and gives the aftermath an authorial paraphrase after the chapter has already landed its pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either cut the closing admonition at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259 or replace it with a line grounded in Harry's own idiom at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259: exposure, counts, timing, leverage, or what reporting will actually set in motion on paper. If Harry addresses motive at all, make it arrive through operational language rather than abstract emotional coaching.
findings.acceptance_test: The revised beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:259 must sound like Harry and add concrete pressure. A reader should hear organizer-accountant logic, not mentor-figure wisdom; if the line still works as a generic moral lesson that any older character could deliver, fail.

findings.problem: The late confrontation drops out of Carmen's interior for thirteen consecutive paragraphs before returning to her legal calculation. In a close-third Carmen chapter, that run starts reading like blocked stage dialogue rather than lived pressure moving through her body and mind.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread brief interior beats back into the stretch spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:145 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:169. Use Carmen's body, perception, or thought at one or more of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:157, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:161 so the Earl-Harry-Marcus turn remains filtered through her rather than through neutral camera coverage.
findings.acceptance_test: Before the legal-calculation beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:171 or its replacement, the revised stretch must contain at least two separate Carmen-internal moments that are unmistakably thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment. If the sequence can still be read straight through as external blocking and dialogue with no direct Carmen interiority for 10 or more paragraphs, fail.

findings.problem: Once Marcus's false note lands, the aftermath keeps restating the same conclusion in cleaner language: he agreed too fast, Agnes says he'll do it anyway, Carmen says he'll do it anyway, Harry says probably, then Carmen worries through a clean count. The scene keeps translating suspicion instead of converting it into a sharper post-vote consequence or countermeasure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-sequence the aftermath from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:217 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275. Preserve the necessary beats: Agnes's refusal to sort, Harry's concession about risk distribution, Carmen's clean count, and Carmen's dread. But make each retained beat add new dramatic work. After the false note at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:197, at least one later beat should change tactics, expose a new operational vulnerability, or alter the Carmen-Harry alliance instead of merely restating that Marcus may lie.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised span beginning after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:197, there may be only one explicit dialogue beat whose function is "Marcus will ignore the vote." Every later beat must add a new fact, action, or relationship shift visible on the page. If two consecutive retained beats can still both be summarized as "they all know he's going to do it anyway," fail.

summary: No continuity or narrative-tense contradiction surfaced, but the chapter still fails shortlist review because the core five-way argument partially flattens into thesis speech, the late exchange drops out of Carmen's interior, and the aftermath over-translates Marcus's false concession instead of sharpening consequence.
